_description: Reseller / White-Label. Three-tier hierarchy: Platform → Resellers → End Customers. Resellers manage their own customers. Permission ceilings prevent resellers from granting more than they have. Best for: white-label platforms, franchise systems, managed service providers, marketplace platforms.
_examples: White-label email — resellers brand it as their own, manage their customer base
Managed hosting — MSPs provision and manage customer environments
Franchise POS — HQ sets menu/pricing ceiling, franchisees customize within bounds

service.name: My Platform
service.description: A white-label platform with reseller channel

tiers.description: Platform org — owns all permissions, manages reseller onboarding
Reseller org — manages their own customer base. Gets a subset of platform permissions (permission ceiling).
End customer org — managed by their reseller. Users sign up here.

isolation._ceiling_note: Resellers cannot grant permissions they don't have. Zanzibar inheritance ensures end customers can never exceed their reseller's permission set.

hosted_login._note: Each end customer gets their own branded login page. Reseller controls branding for their customers.

onboarding._note: Platform admin creates resellers. Resellers create their own customers. End users self-register or are invited.

service_accounts._note: Platform service accounts for platform-level automation. Reseller service accounts for reseller-level API access and customer provisioning.

delegation._note: Optional. Enable if reseller admins need to delegate customer management to support staff.

sso._note: Optional. Resellers may have their own corporate IdP. End customers under enterprise resellers may also need SSO.

teams._note: Optional. Enable if end customers need sub-groups (e.g., departments within a customer org).

_zero_config_note: Three-tier Zanzibar inheritance: Platform → Reseller → Customer. Each level inherits from its parent. Permission ceilings are structural — a reseller's customers can never exceed the reseller's own permission set.
